questions.q: **Scotland Yard** is the headquarters of which police force?
questions.fact: The Met's original 1829 headquarters backed onto a street called Great Scotland Yard.
Interpol connects police forces of member countries; it has no arrest powers of its own.

questions.q: In the United States, the **Miranda warning** informs an arrested person of…
choices: The right to remain silent and to an attorney
The officer's name and badge number
questions.fact: It stems from Miranda v. Arizona (1966).

questions.q: What is the **emergency number** in the Netherlands (and across the EU)?
questions.fact: 112 works in every EU country; 911 is North America.

questions.q: What color are **police emergency lights** in the Netherlands?
questions.fact: Dutch emergency services run blue lights; red-and-blue is typically American.
The Federal Bureau of Investigation is the principal federal investigative agency.

questions.q: The **RCMP** — the "Mounties" — are the national police of which country?
questions.fact: The Royal Canadian Mounted Police, famous for their red serge uniforms.
Walking the beat = patrolling one's assigned territory.

questions.q: The **Carabinieri** are a national gendarmerie of which country?
questions.fact: Italy's Carabinieri are a military corps with police duties.
From fingerprints to DNA — science in service of the case.
